{"step_by_step_thinking": "The child has an Ellis class-III fracture in 11, which means the pulp is exposed. The first step in managing this type of fracture is to take a radiograph to assess the extent of the fracture and the condition of the pulp. Cvek's pulpotomy is a procedure that involves amputating the pulp 2 mm below the exposure site, which is the treatment of choice for young teeth with open apices when the pulp capping is contraindicated. Given that the pulp is exposed, Cvek's pulpotomy is the appropriate initial treatment.", "answer_choice": "A"}
